top_signals.title: Oil Heads for Weekly Loss as Hormuz Tanker Traffic Rebounds
top_signals.summary: Crude oil prices were on course for a sharp weekly loss amid multiple reports about a strong rebound in tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz. At the time of writing, Brent crude was trading at $73.78 per barrel, and West Texas Intermediate was changing hands for $70.53, even after the news of an Iranian strike on a commercial vessel in Hormuz that, Tehran suggested, had used a route not approved by the Iranian authorities. The strike promoted a 2% reversal in oil price movement, but it was not enough to quash optimism about tanker traffic overall.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Oil slides nearly 2% as markets look past fresh Iran tensions and focus on supply outlook - CNBC

top_signals.title: ON Semiconductor strikes $7 billion deal for Synaptics in physical AI push
top_signals.summary: ON Semiconductor said the deal bumps up its total addressable market by $30 billion, to $243 billion by 2030.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin briefly dips below $59,000 as macro pressures drag crypto, Asian equities lower
top_signals.summary: Asian equities also fell sharply Friday morning, with South Korea's Kospi losing over 8% and triggering a circuit breaker.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: SoftBank plunges 13%, SK Hynix slides 10% as Asia tech rout tracks declines in the U.S.
top_signals.summary: SoftBank Group plunged, leading a broad selloff in Asian technology stocks amid mounting concerns over the rising cost of artificial intelligence infrastructure.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: House backs $1.55 billion to revive E-7 Wedgetail, spares Navy Hawkeye
top_signals.summary: The White House budget office wants to raid a Navy radar-plane account and a classified Air Force line to pull the E-7 Wedgetail back from the dead.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Women’s rugby in England is way ahead, and the RFU deserves credit
top_signals.summary: In a phrase many thought a rugby fan would never utter: The RFU, the governing body of the sport in England, is doing a world-leading job. Hold the front page, it surely cannot be true given recent controversies surrounding bonuses and the structure of the men’s game below Prem Rugby. But it is true. But [...]
